sidebar.subtitleAITools: Where you read team memory back out

auth.tagline: Align every AI tool on your team with your team's actual workflow.
auth.subtagline: Your team uses Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT — but each AI sees a different slice of what your team's actually doing. We align them all with one source of team workflow, so your AIs stop giving different answers.
auth.stubModeBody: No Supabase project is configured. Any email + 6+ char password will sign you in locally.
auth.localFootnote: Memory dir lives on this machine. Nothing leaves until you wire a Sink.
auth.realAuthHint: Real auth (Supabase). Stub-mode acceptable until CEO unblocks keys.

today.title: Today
today.footer: Sources fan out into ~/.tangerine-memory · refreshes every 5 min

activity.emptyBody: Connect a source to start the feed.

memory.title: Your team's memory
memory.subtitle: Tangerine listens in every corner of your team's comms, structures it into team memory, and keeps your team — and their AI tools — on the same page.
memory.preMeetingBriefBody: Tangerine will brief you 5 min before any meeting that touches this decision.
memory.howItWorksTitle: Tangerine is your team's Chief of Staff
memory.howItWorksBody: captures every corner of comms, briefs the team, briefs their AI. We never run an LLM ourselves; your existing Claude / ChatGPT / Cursor read team memory through Sinks.
memory.copiedToast: Copied to clipboard. Paste into ChatGPT / Claude.
memory.comingV16Sameset: Coming v1.6 — full team alignment dashboard
memory.comingV16Brief: Coming v1.6 — your team's morning brief, auto-generated from yesterday's captures
memory.comingV16AutoCoS: Coming v1.6 — Tangerine briefs your team and their AI tools throughout the day
memory.noSourceCta: Same-screen rate: 0% — connect a Source so your team and their AI start aligning.
memory.withSourceCta: Your AI sees {{meetings}} meetings · {{decisions}} decisions · {{people}} people · 0% of Slack / Linear / Notion (Sources land v1.6+).
memory.emptyBody: Connect a source and Tangerine will start writing meeting / decision / thread notes here. Until then this view is intentionally quiet.

graph.emptyBody: Add a note to see the graph.

coThinker.title: Team brain
coThinker.subtitle: Your team's shared brain. Reads new notes every few minutes and writes its observations into the doc below.
coThinker.errorReadFallback: Could not read team brain doc.
coThinker.emptyTitle: Team brain hasn't started thinking yet.
coThinker.emptyBody: Your team brain runs in the background — every 5 minutes when the app is open. It reads new captures from your sources, writes its observations here, and surfaces what your team should pay attention to.
coThinker.honorEdit: The team brain will honor your edit on the next sync.
coThinker.honorEditToast: Brain doc saved. The team brain honors your edit on the next sync.

explainer.heading: This is your team's shared brain.
explainer.intro: Team brain is your team's shared memory. Every 5 minutes (or 30 minutes when you're idle) it reads your team's sources and writes a markdown doc right here.
explainer.readBody: what it's thinking — every section is a markdown card.
explainer.editBody: the doc to steer it; the team brain honors your edit on the next sync.
explainer.diffBody: changes over time — it's just a file in your memory dir.
explainer.noSubBody: — runs through your existing AI tool session.
explainer.readyBody: Click Initialize to fire the first sync. The brain doc will render below within a few seconds.

canvas.title: Canvas
canvas.subtitle: Per-project ideation surface. Sticky notes you and your team throw, with Tangerine joining as a peer.
canvas.emptyBody: Each canvas is per-project. Pick a project and open its canvas — sticky notes you and your team throw show up here, with Tangerine as a peer.
canvas.emptyFooter: Open a project, then come back here — its canvas slug will appear.

reviews.title: Reviews
reviews.subtitle: Team brain proposals waiting on team vote. 2/3 approval auto-promotes.
reviews.emptyOpen: No open reviews. The team brain will propose decisions on the next sync.

review.tabsSubtitle: Decisions waiting on a vote. 2/3 approval ratifies.

pending.title: Inbox zero.
pending.body: Nothing is waiting on your vote. Atoms a teammate proposes will land here.

proposed.title: Nothing proposed yet.
proposed.body: When you propose an atom for review, it will appear here with vote progress.

ratified.title: No ratified decisions yet.
ratified.body: Decisions that pass quorum land here as the team's audit trail.

rejected.title: No rejected decisions.
rejected.body: Atoms that fail quorum or hit their deadline land here.

review.rosterEmpty: No teammates besides @{{user}} on the roster yet. Add a teammate to propose.

comments.placeholder: Add a comment. Use @ to mention a teammate.
comments.empty: No comments yet. Click a paragraph to start a thread.

marketplace.title: Marketplace
marketplace.subtitle: Vertical templates published by the community + self-shipped reference packs. One-click install merges team brain prompts, source configs, canvas templates, and suggestion rules into your team memory.
marketplace.emptyFilteredHint: Clear the search or pick a different vertical.
marketplace.launchTitle: Coming live when CEO triggers launch gate
marketplace.launchBody: Public marketplace launches when both conditions are met: 5,000 OSS installs over a rolling 30-day window AND ≥1 self-shipped vertical template internally validated for ≥30 days. Until then, the listing below is preview-only.

github.title: GitHub
github.intro: Pulls PR threads, review comments, and merge decisions into
github.introTail: . With writeback ON, finalised decisions in
github.introTail2: get posted back as a markdown comment on the linked PR.
github.captureBody: Capture for GitHub runs from the
github.captureBodyTail: Node package (separate process). Once authorised it reads PRs + issues for the repos you opt into. This pane shows whether the connector is currently writing notes.
github.captureNote: Capture configuration lives outside this pane (per-repo cursors live in
github.captureNoteTail: ). This page intentionally focuses on writeback — Phase 2-A.
github.writebackIntroTail: and posts a markdown comment on the linked PR / issue whenever a new decision file with
github.writebackIntroTail2: is written. The comment format is shown below — your team sees a single 🍊 message, unmistakably from Tangerine.
github.postHint: Reuses your existing GitHub OAuth token. Read-only when OFF.
github.loginHint: The login we use to look up your token in the OS keychain. Defaults to the account you signed in with during onboarding.
github.logEmpty: No writebacks yet. Finalize a decision in
github.writebackOn: GitHub writeback ON. Decisions will post back automatically.
github.writebackOff: GitHub writeback OFF. Capture continues unchanged.
github.noConfig: No ~/.tmi/config.yaml yet. Finish the Discord setup first so the schema is initialised.
github.modalTitle: Post comments to GitHub on Tangerine's behalf?
github.modalBody:
When enabled, Tangerine will post decision summaries as markdown comments on the linked PR or issue. Each comment is automated — no per-message confirm.

This is a one-time confirm. Disable any time.

linear.title: Linear
linear.intro: Pulls issues, comments, and project state into
linear.introTail: . With writeback ON, finalised decisions in
linear.introTail2: open a fresh "decision recorded" issue (state Done, label
linear.introTail3: ) in the team the original issue belongs to.
linear.captureBody: Capture for Linear runs from the
linear.captureBodyTail: Node package (separate process). Issues, comments, and project state changes become notes in your memory dir.
linear.captureNoteTail: . This page focuses on writeback — Phase 2-A.
linear.writebackIntroTail: and creates a Linear issue whenever a new decision file with
linear.writebackIntroTail2: is written. Issue title comes from the decision's
linear.writebackIntroTail3: frontmatter; body is the full decision markdown.
linear.keyHintConfigured: Key is set in your .env. Replace it by typing a new key here.
linear.keyHintMissing: Get a personal API key from Linear → Settings → API.
linear.writebackOn: Linear writeback ON. Decisions will open issues automatically.
linear.writebackOff: Linear writeback OFF. Capture continues unchanged.
linear.modalTitle: Open Linear issues on Tangerine's behalf?
linear.modalBody:
When enabled, Tangerine will open a fresh "decision recorded" issue (state Done, label tangerine-decision) in the team for every finalized decision. Each issue is automated — no per-message confirm.

This is a one-time confirm. Disable any time.

slack.title: Slack
slack.intro: The Slack source captures messages into your memory dir. Writeback closes the loop: 5min before a meeting we post a brief; once the meeting wraps, we post the decision summary back to the same channel.
slack.preBriefHint: When a calendar event matches a memory note, post a markdown brief to the linked channel 5 minutes before it starts.
slack.summaryLabel: Post decision summary after meetings end
slack.summaryHint: When a meeting note flips to status: finalized, post a summary (decisions + action items) to the linked channel.
slack.fallbackHint: Channel id (e.g. C0PROJECTROADMAP) we post to when the note's own slack_channel field is unset. Leave blank to require notes to specify their own channel.
slack.testHint: Smoke-test the writeback path. The Rust side surfaces `slack_token_missing` if the bot token isn't in the keychain.
slack.modalTitle: Post to Slack on Tangerine's behalf?
slack.modalBody:
When enabled, Tangerine will post pre-meeting briefs and decision summaries to your team's Slack channel. Each post is automated — no per-message confirm.

This is a one-time confirm. Disable any time.

external.title: External
external.intro: Subscribe to RSS feeds and podcasts; paste any YouTube URL or article link. Every captured item lands as a markdown note under
external.introTail: . Personal vault — never synced to the team.
external.rssHint: Daily fetch in the background; one note per new entry.
external.podcastHint: Subscribe to the RSS; transcripts are off by default (heavy on CPU).
external.youtubeHint: Paste any YouTube URL — we capture the transcript via captions.
external.articleHint: Paste any web URL — we extract the main content as a note.

settings.title: Settings
settings.unsavedHint: Unsaved changes are discarded on close.
settings.advancedHint: Adapters, Team, Advanced, and Language tabs are hidden by default. Show them when you need to tune the underlying mechanism.

general.meetingsRepoHint: Git repo where meetings/ subdirs live.
general.tzHint: Locked to Asia/Shanghai per project default. T6 will surface a picker if anyone asks.

language.label: Language
language.subtitle: UI language. Memory content is never translated.

agi.participationBody: Master switch. When off, no inline reactions, no sync, no system tray. Your team brain pauses.
agi.sensitivityBody: One knob for how often Tangerine speaks up. Lower = quieter, higher = chattier. The very top end is "alerts only" — a sensitivity of 100 means only the very-high-confidence reactions surface.
agi.advancedHint: Volume band, raw confidence threshold, per-channel mutes. The sensitivity slider above derives all three; only open this if you need finer control.
agi.volumeBody: The whole app is a chat surface. Your team brain may surface a tiny inline reaction — a 🍊 dot in the page margin — when you type into any input. Volume controls how often.
agi.thresholdBody: Reactions below this confidence never surface, regardless of volume. Default 0.70.
agi.channelsBody: Disable ambient reactions on individual surfaces without flipping the global volume.
agi.dismissBody: When you dismiss a reaction, that surface is muted for 24h. Currently {{count}} surface silenced.
agi.dismissBody_other: When you dismiss a reaction, that surface is muted for 24h. Currently {{count}} surfaces silenced.
agi.suppressionBodyZero: Suggestions you've dismissed 3+ times for the same note or surface get silenced for 30 days. Nothing is currently suppressed.
agi.suppressionBodyOne: Suggestions you've dismissed 3+ times for the same note or surface get silenced for 30 days. {{count}} entry is silenced.
agi.suppressionBodyOther: Suggestions you've dismissed 3+ times for the same note or surface get silenced for 30 days. {{count}} entries are silenced.
agi.telemetryBody: Tangerine logs every meaningful action (route changes, note opens, dismisses, …) so the suggestion engine can detect patterns. All events stay local — nothing syncs to the cloud. Files older than 90 days are auto-pruned.
agi.telemetryClean: Telemetry already clean — nothing to clear.
agi.telemetryCleared_one: Telemetry cleared ({{count}} day of events removed).
agi.telemetryCleared_other: Telemetry cleared ({{count}} days of events removed).
agi.volumeSilent: No inline reactions ever. Team brain still runs in the background and tray, just not in the margin.
agi.volumeQuiet: Default. High-confidence reactions only (≥ your confidence floor + the volume floor). Most input events stay silent.
agi.volumeChatty: Lower bar. Surfaces lower-confidence hunches too. Useful when you actively want the team brain to push back more often.
agi.bandAlerts: Alerts only — only the very-high-confidence reactions surface.

adapters.title: Output adapters
adapters.subtitle: Where applied diffs land. v1.5 ships only the Claude Code adapter.

advanced.debugBody: Zips app log, tmi log, sanitized config, and the last 5 status.yaml files. Send this to
advanced.debugBodyTail: with a description of the issue.
advanced.aboutHint: Auto-update infrastructure ships, but the manifest endpoint is empty for the dogfood beta. v1.5.1 will be the first auto-pulled release.

personalAgents.heading: Personal AI tool capture
personalAgents.intro: Tangerine reads your local AI tool conversation logs and writes one note per conversation under
personalAgents.introTail: . Strict opt-in — every source is OFF until you turn it on. Notes stay on this machine; the personal vault is git-ignored and never syncs to the team repo.
personalAgents.syncTooltipNotDetected: Source not detected on this machine
personalAgents.noCaptures: No captures yet — flip a toggle and click "Sync now" to start.

billing.title: Billing
billing.stubBannerTitle: Coming live when CEO unblocks Stripe / Supabase keys.
billing.stubBannerBody: Right now this page runs in stub mode: state transitions persist locally but no real charge happens. Frontend & IPC contracts are locked, so the live swap is just an env var flip.
billing.trialAddPaymentBody: Add payment now to keep Tangerine Cloud running. Cloud sync, team git mirror, and shared briefs stop at expiry — local OSS keeps working.
billing.trialCriticalBody: Add payment now or Cloud features pause at expiry.
billing.trialRelaxed: {{remaining}} left in trial · No card needed
billing.expiredBody: Cloud sync, team git mirror, and shared briefs are paused until you add payment. Local memory tree + OSS path keep working — no data loss.
billing.cancelled: Subscription cancelled. Re-subscribe at any time — your local memory tree is preserved.
billing.confirmCancel: Cancel subscription? Cloud features stop at the end of the current period.

onboarding.needGitTitle: We need git on this machine first
onboarding.needGitBody: Tangerine syncs your team memory through git. It's a 60-second install and you only do it once. Most developers already have it.
onboarding.signInGithubBody: We opened the GitHub login page. Type this code:
onboarding.heroTitle: Solo + a connected source = working CoS in 30 seconds.
onboarding.heroBody: Pick Solo, connect Discord / GitHub / Slack, and the daemon starts writing your team memory while you keep working. Switch to Team mode from Settings whenever you're ready to share.
onboarding.pickerTitle: Where will your team's memory live?
onboarding.pickerBody: Tangerine stores meetings, decisions, and AI context as markdown files your team can share via git.
onboarding.createNewBody: Tangerine creates a private repo, adds a starter folder, and gives you an invite link for your team. About 10 seconds.
onboarding.useExistingBody: Paste a clone URL. Useful when your team already has a memory repo from another machine.
onboarding.soloBody: Memory stays in ~/.tangerine-memory. Switch to team mode any time.
onboarding.soloToast: Memory stays on this machine. Switch to team mode any time from Settings.
onboarding.pasteCloneFirst: Paste a GitHub clone URL first.

inbox.title: Pending alerts
inbox.emptyBody: Nothing pending. The daemon hasn't found stale decisions, overdue work, or knowledge gaps.

aiExtracted.headerTodo: {{author}} has a TODO for you in {{vendor}}

inbox.subtitle: Mentions, review requests, and replies your teammates sent your way.
inbox.emptyTabBody: When a teammate @mentions you, asks for a review, or replies to a thread you're in, it shows up here.

alignment.title: Same-screen rate
alignment.heroFootnote: Share of captured notes viewed by every tracked team member. Live from cursor diff in
alignment.behindHealthy: Everyone tracked has viewed every captured note. Same-screen rate is healthy.

errors.internal_io: Couldn't save. Disk may be full or permissions blocked.
errors.parse: File is corrupt. See log for details.
errors.offline: Offline — changes will sync when connection returns.

setupWizard.welcomeTitle: Set up your team brain in 60 seconds
setupWizard.welcomeBody: Tangerine uses your existing AI tools to power the team brain. We'll detect what you have and configure it automatically.
setupWizard.welcomeHint: No new account, no extra subscription. We just teach Tangerine which AI session to talk to.
setupWizard.detectSubtitle: We'll suggest the easiest path. You can pick something else if you prefer.
setupWizard.alreadyConfigured: Tangerine entry already in mcp.json — restart your editor to activate.
setupWizard.noEditorFound: No MCP-capable editor (Cursor / Claude Code / Codex / Windsurf) detected.
setupWizard.installSomethingHint: We didn't find an editor or Ollama on this machine.
setupWizard.noChannelSelected: Pick a channel on the previous step.
setupWizard.mcpInstructions: We'll write the Tangerine MCP entry to {{path}}. After it's saved, restart {{tool}} so the bridge comes up.
setupWizard.restartHint: Restart {{tool}} for the bridge to come up.
setupWizard.pasteHint: Paste this into {{path}}, then restart your editor.
setupWizard.restartGateTitle: Restart {{tool}} so the MCP bridge can connect
setupWizard.restartStep3: Wait ~10 seconds for Tangerine MCP server to spawn

errors.mcp_sampler_not_registered: {{tool}} hasn't connected to Tangerine yet. Did you fully close + reopen {{tool}} after auto-configure? It needs a restart to load the new MCP config.
errors.mcp_sampler_timeout: {{tool}} connected but didn't respond within 10s. Try again or check {{tool}}'s logs.
errors.mcp_sampler_disconnected: {{tool}} dropped the connection mid-request. Reopen {{tool}} and try again.
errors.mcp_host_rejected: {{tool}} returned an error. Check its console for details.
errors.mcp_bridge_internal: Internal MCP bridge error. Restart Tangerine and try again.
errors.ollama_client_init: Couldn't initialize the HTTP client for Ollama. Restart Tangerine and try again.
errors.ollama_connection_refused: Ollama is not running on 127.0.0.1:11434. Start Ollama (`ollama serve`) or pick a different channel.
errors.ollama_http_status: Ollama is running but the model isn't pulled. Run `ollama pull llama3.1:8b-instruct-q4_K_M` then retry.
errors.ollama_parse_error: Ollama responded but the body wasn't valid JSON. The version on this machine may be too old.
errors.browser_ext_not_implemented: Browser extension channel is not yet implemented (coming v2.0).
errors.all_channels_exhausted: Every channel failed. Open Setup again and pick one to fix.
errors.unknown: Unknown error. Check Tangerine's logs for details.

setupWizard.ollamaInstructions: Tangerine will use your local Ollama (model: {{model}}). Make sure it's running on 127.0.0.1:11434.
setupWizard.browserInstructions: The browser-ext channel is still in stub mode but the wizard will mark this channel ready so you can keep moving.
setupWizard.installInstructions: Install one of these to give Tangerine an LLM channel. Both are free.
setupWizard.testSubtitle: Sending a one-line test prompt through the channel you picked.
setupWizard.doneBody: Your team brain will start thinking on the next sync (~5 min).
setupWizard.bannerText: Set up your AI inference channel — your team brain is currently offline.
setupWizard.heartbeatToastTitle: Sync failed: no AI inference channel set up.
setupWizard.heartbeatToastBody: Tangerine needs an AI inference channel before the brain can think.

onboardingChat.initialPrompt: Hi, I'm Tangerine. To set you up, I need to know two things: (a) your primary AI tool (Cursor / Claude Code / Codex / Windsurf / Ollama), and (b) optionally a GitHub repo for team sync. Just tell me in your own words — e.g. "primary=Claude Code, repo=github.com/me/team-private".
onboardingChat.inputPlaceholder: Tell me what you want to set up…
onboardingChat.setupComplete: Setup complete. Try asking me anything about your team — e.g. "What did we decide about pricing last week?"
onboardingChat.fallbackHint: Prefer a form? Press Cmd+K and pick "Set up LLM channel".
onboardingChat.scopePrompt: Hi! Are you setting up just for yourself, or for a team?
onboardingChat.scopeSoloAck: Got it — solo setup. Tell me your primary AI tool (Cursor / Claude Code / Codex / Windsurf / Ollama). I'll skip the team-roster step.
onboardingChat.scopeTeamAck: Got it — team setup. Tell me your primary AI tool and a GitHub repo for the shared memory dir. E.g. "primary=Claude Code, repo=github.com/me/team-private".

welcome.title: Your team's shared AI memory, in 30 seconds.
welcome.subtitle: Four things to know before you start.

card1.title: No new AI subscription
card1.body: Use your existing Cursor / Claude Pro / ChatGPT. Your team's shared AI memory runs on the AI you already pay for.

card2.title: Your team's memory is a markdown doc you own
card2.body: Readable, editable, git-able. Open `co-thinker.md` to see exactly what the team brain is thinking — edit it to steer.

card3.title: Cross-vendor visibility
card3.body: We read your team's notes from every AI tool they use (Cursor, Codex, Claude, ChatGPT, Devin, Replit, Copilot, Windsurf, Gemini, Ollama). The full picture, in one place.

card4.title: 10 AI tools aligned
card4.body: The sidebar shows which tools we've wired in and which one is your starred ⭐ primary. Add more in seconds.

card5.title: Your data, your machines.
card5.body: Tangerine reads your AI tools' local files + your team's git repo. Tokens stored in your OS keychain. 0 KB sent to Tangerine ever unless you opt into Cloud sync.

welcome.tourReplayHint: Re-shows the 4-card intro overlay you saw on first launch.
welcome.heartbeatNoChannelTitle: Sync failed: no AI inference channel is set up.
welcome.heartbeatNoChannelBody: To enable your team brain, [a] install Cursor and add `TANGERINE_SAMPLING_BRIDGE=1` to your MCP config, or [b] install Ollama at the default port.

git.stateNotInitialized: Memory dir is not git-tracked. Click to initialize.
git.stateClean: Up to date with team. Last pull: {{when}}.
git.stateConflict: Conflict: resolve in your shell. Click for details.
git.bannerTitle: Tangerine works best when your memory dir is git-tracked.
git.bannerBody: Git lets your team share decisions, brain doc, and notes via a shared remote.
git.settingsResetWarning: Removes the origin remote and unstages all local commits. The .git history is preserved.

stat.title: Daily snapshot
stat.body: Atoms captured, teammates watched, AI tools active. The shape of your team's day at a glance.

hero.title: Ask anything about your team
hero.body: Search team memory or ask the brain a question. Try one of the sample queries below the box.

decisions.title: Recent decisions
decisions.body: Decisions your team has ratified. Click any to open the full atom.

activity.title: Live activity
activity.body: Real-time stream of what your AI tools are writing into team memory.

memory.title: Memory tree
memory.body: Browse every atom in an Obsidian-style file tree. Filter by vendor, date, or search.

brain.title: Team brain
brain.body: Your team's AGI-summarized brain doc. Editable markdown — git-trackable, fully yours.

tour.completeBody: Your real data lands as your team uses AI tools. Press ? any time for shortcuts.

tryThis.title: Did you know?
tryThis.cmdK: Press {{kbd}} to open the search palette from any route — it indexes every memory atom.
tryThis.brainEdit: Edit the brain doc directly at ~/.tangerine-memory/team/co-thinker.md — your edits steer the next sync.
tryThis.starTool: Star your primary AI tool in Settings → AI tools so the brain knows which session to talk to.
tryThis.memoryGit: Your memory dir is a git repo — diff every change with `git log` inside ~/.tangerine-memory.
tryThis.tourReplay: Settings → General has a 'Show welcome tour again' link if you want to replay the intro.
tryThis.shortcuts: Press ? to open the global keyboard shortcuts cheatsheet.

today.title: Nothing captured yet
today.body: Your team's AI tools haven't written anything yet. Connect a source or run the welcome tour to see how it works.

memory.title: Memory is empty
memory.body: Connect a source — Slack, GitHub, Linear — or your AI tools' history will populate here.

brain.title: Team brain hasn't started thinking yet
brain.body: Click Initialize to fire the first sync and let the brain read your team memory.

canvas.title: Drop your first sticky note
canvas.body: Pick a project and open its canvas — Tangerine joins as a peer once you start.

reviews.title: No decisions to review yet
reviews.body: The team brain proposes decisions on each sync. Once two-thirds of teammates approve, they auto-promote.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.subtitle: Tangerine runs 100% on your machines by default. Receipts below.
privacy.telemetryHint: Default ON. Helps us see which features get used. No content, no identifiers.
privacy.verifyZero: 0 calls to Tangerine servers in last hour
privacy.verifyNonZero: calls to Tangerine servers in last hour

local.whisper_transcription: Whisper transcription (faster-whisper bundled, runs CPU local)
local.mcp_sampling: MCP sampling (uses your editor's LLM, not ours)
local.discord_bot_subprocess: Discord bot subprocess (Node.js on your machine)
local.ai_tool_conversations: AI tool conversation files (read locally, never uploaded)

egress.git_push_user_remote: Git push to your specified remote (your data → your server)
egress.auto_updater_check: Auto-updater check (version comparison only, no data)
egress.cloud_sync_optional: If Cloud opted in: encrypted brain sync to Tangerine servers

lark.title: Lark / Feishu
lark.subtitle: CN priority. Pulls messages from team chats you're in.

teams.title: Microsoft Teams
teams.subtitle: Channel + chat messages from teams you belong to.

slack.title: Slack
slack.subtitle: Messages from channels you're in.
slack.appTokenLabel: App token (xapp-…) — optional, for socket mode
